jobs.title: SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
jobs.description:
**body { font\-family:** "Source Sans Pro",sans\-serif; } caption { visibility: hidden; } /\*h3 { all: unset; }\*/ /\*p { all: unset; }\*/ .primaryPostingHeader { color: \#FDB81E; font\-size: 22pt; } .secondaryPostingHeader { font\-size: 20pt; color: \#02BFE7; } .postingHeader { font\-weight: 200; font\-size: 18pt; margin\-top: 15px; margin\-bottom: 15px; } .col { display: inline\-block; min\-height: 1px; } .col\-twothirds { width: 66%; } .col\-onethird { width: 33%; } .col\-half { width: 49%; } .paddingTop10 { padding\-top: 10px; } .jcHead { display: inline\-block; width: 100%; background\-color: \#323A45; color: white; } .jcHead\-RightPane { vertical\-align: top; min\-width: 150px; } .jcBand { padding\-top: 10px; padding\-bottom: 10px; padding\-left: 35px; padding\-right: 35px; } .jcBand: nth\-child(2n\+1\) { background\-color: whitesmoke; } .stateSealLogo { display: none; padding\-left: 50px; padding\-bottom: 20px; } @media only screen and (min\-width: 1300px) { .stateSealLogo { display: inline\-block; } } @media only screen and (max\-width: 1024px) { .col\-half { width: 100%; } .col\-onethird { width: 100%; } .col\-twothirds { width: 100%; } } .boldHeading { margin\-top: 15px; font\-weight: bold; } .wHeaderCol { width: 125px; }
  
  

**Job Posting:** Unit Chief
  
  

Department of Toxic Substances Control
  
  

JC\-523280 \- Unit Chief
  
SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
  
  

$10,854\.00 \- $13,493\.00 per Month
  
New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.
  
  

**Final Filing Date:** 7/15/2026
  
  

Job Description and Duties
  
  

Are you an experienced leader looking for a rewarding technical career opportunity? This position supervises a multidisciplinary team of scientists, engineers and geologists responsible for resolving complex landfill projects. If you enjoy partnering with technical leads to developing effective solutions to challenging issues, we encourage you to apply!
  
  

As a Senior Environmental Scientist (Supervisory) you will have the opportunity to:
  
  

Provide direction and training to staff who oversee environmental investigations and cleanup activities at properties where hazardous materials, substances or waste were released into the environment
  
Ensure that the requirements of legal mandates, which DTSC is party to, are implemented in an appropriate manner
  
Provide guidance on staff’s review of submitted site cleanup related documents (e.g., Phase I, Preliminary Endangerment Assessment/Resource Conservation and Recovery Act Facility Assessment, Engineering Evaluation/Cost Analysis, Site Characterization work plans and reports, Remedial Investigation/ Feasibility Studies, Sampling and Analysis Plans, Human Health and Ecological Risk Assessment, etc.), field work oversight, regulatory decision making, and communication with responsible parties, project proponents, external agencies, and the public
  
Monitor staff’s review of site cleanup\-related documents for compliance with applicable standards and requirements, conduct oversight of field work consistent with DTSC approved work plans, and ensure remediation work meets federal and state standards
  
Provide technical consultation and support to staff on most complex technical issues including investigations, data analysis, removal actions, remedial actions, and implementation of any repair, modification, or improvements to the site
  
Oversee and assist staff in evaluating appropriate sampling and mitigation techniques, based on state of practice scientific and engineering practices, or as appropriate, uses innovative technologies to address unique and/or complex problems
  
  

**Desirable Qualifications We Look For:**  

Strong leadership skills
  
Experience with CERCLA environmental cleanup process
  
Strong communication skills
  
Strong problem\-solving skills
  
Experience with large/complex projects
  
Experience with soil gas and groundwater remediation projects
  
  

Working Conditions
  
  

This position is not eligible for visa sponsorship. Applicants must be authorized to work in the United States without the need for visa sponsorship by the start date of employment. The Department of Toxic Substances Control does not participate in E\-Verify for employment authorization purposes.
  
  

A hybrid schedule may be available for this position.
  
  

DTSC \- Diversity, Equity, Inclusion and Belonging (DEIB)
  
  

Position Details
  
**Job Code \#:** JC\-523280
  
  

**Position \#(s):**  

810\-591\-0764\-001
  
**Working Title:** Unit Chief
  
**Classification:** SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
  
$10,854\.00 \- $13,493\.00
  
New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.
  
  

**\# of Positions:** 1
  
**Work Location:** Sacramento County
  
**Telework:** Hybrid
  
**Job Type:** Permanent, Full Time
  
**Facility:** Cal Center Regional Office
  
  

Department Information
  
  

This position is located at DTSC’s Cal Center Regional office in Sacramento, CA.
  
  

The Department of Toxic Substances Control (DTSC) is a member of the boards, departments, and offices (BDOs) within the California Environmental Protection Agency (CalEPA).
  
  

**Our Mission:** To protect California’s people, communities, and environment from toxic substances, to enhance economic vitality by restoring contaminated land, and to compel manufacturers to make safer consumer products.
  
  

**Our Vision:** All of California is thriving in a healthy environment.
  
  

**Our Core Values:** DTSC values diversity, equity, and inclusion throughout the organization. We foster an environment where employees from a variety of backgrounds, cultures, and personal experiences are welcomed and can thrive. We believe the diversity of our employees is essential to inspiring innovative solutions. Together we further ou

jobs.title: Staff Geologist
jobs.description:
Job Description: **Take Your Environmental Career to the Next Level**
Kleinfelder, a Forbes' Top Employer for Engineers in North America, is committed to a people\-first culture, technical excellence, and creating opportunities where employees can thrive!  

Are you ready to be challenged, make a difference, and experience professional growth in your career? Kleinfelder's Environmental team is looking for you! From environmental planning, permitting, remediation, and site characterization to chemical data management and hydrogeology, Kleinfelder offers a variety of services designed to provide technically appropriate and cost\-effective solutions.  

**Step Into Your Next Role****Kleinfelder is currently seeking a Staff Geologist to serve Southern California and surrounding areas.** Working out of Kleinfelder’s San Diego office and serving the region, the successful candidate will perform environmental due diligence and site investigations / field sampling services. We are seeking a candidate that is motivated, can work independently, has strong technical writing skills, and enjoys working a mix of office and outdoors. The candidate will conduct environmental site work and field sampling that include, but are not limited to, the following:

* Performing and supporting environmental field work / analysis including soil boring logging, sampling, and testing.
* Conducting site inspections as part of Phase I Environmental Site Assessments (ESAs) and compliance audits.
* Preparation of proposals, cost estimates, plans, and technical specifications, including day\-to\-day client and stakeholder correspondence.
* Review and interpret environmental data and analytical laboratory results.
* Prepare technical reports, field reports, and internal / external correspondence.
* Soil boring and monitoring well installation.
* Soil, sediment, surface water, and groundwater screening and sampling.
* Storm water, wastewater, and influent/effluent process water sampling.
* Oversight of subcontractors and vendors performing drilling, laboratory analytical services, maintenance, and excavation activities.

The candidate must have an appreciation for the importance of adhering to a safety culture that believes that all accidents are preventable. Additionally, the candidate will be expected to follow written technical procedures and document field activities commensurate with individual project expectations.

 **Qualifications:**

* Bachelor's.degree in geology \- Master's preferred
* GIT certification
* 3\-6 years of experience in the consulting business with demonstrated experience in geology
* Experience with drilling including soil logging, sample collection and drilling subcontractor management.
* Experience in the preparation of technical reports, proposals, and cost estimates.
* Ability for meeting and exceeding deadlines while maintaining project budgets and regulatory compliance status.
* Ability to employ loss prevention and quality control systems.
* Excellent oral and written communication skills, positive attitude, and ability to work both independently and within groups.
* 40\-hour OSHA HAZWOPER health and safety training \- preferred

Following an offer, candidates will be required to satisfactorily complete employment reference checks, verification of professional licensure and/or educational credentials, a motor vehicle report (MVR), and a comprehensive background check. **Move Forward with Kleinfelder:** Kleinfelder and its’ subsidiaries and affiliates, has been connecting great people to the best work since 1961\. We are engineers, scientists, and construction professionals providing solutions that improve our clients’ transportation, water, energy, and other private infrastructure. As a responsive, cross\-disciplinary team of bright, curious, and innovative problem\-solvers, we are dedicated to doing the right thing, every day, on every project from over 110 offices in the US, Canada, and Australia. Connecting great people to the best work is our purpose – together, we deliver. **Progress with an Employer that Values You**
Kleinfelder, and its’ subsidiaries and affiliates, is an inclusive organization free from discrimination. We are a stronger organization when we are a diverse workforce and believe that through diversity, equity, and inclusion comes creativity, innovation, and unity. We are proud to offer the following: ***Benefits:***Kleinfelder, and its’ subsidiaries and affiliates, offers an excellent compensation and benefits package, including: medical, dental, vision, life insurance, 401(k) plan, and paid holidays. The expected salary range for the position is displayed in accordance with the California Pay Transparency Law. Final agreed upon compensation is based upon individual qualifications and experience. Salary Range: $61,734 \- $82,934\. ***Career Development:***We are committed to investing in the professional development of our staff, offering each employee every opportunity to grow, develop, and take control of their career paths. We support these efforts through reimbursements for continuing education as well as many of the expenses associated with trainings and certifications, and opportunities for career development through our internal Mentoring Program. ***Equal Opportunity:***Kleinfelder, and its’ subsidiaries and affiliates, is an Equal Opportunity Employer – Minorities/Women/Disabled/Veterans. (Compliant with the new VEVRAA and Section 503 rules)**NOTICE TO THIRD PARTY AGENCIES**
Please note that Kleinfelder, and its’ subsidiaries and affiliates, does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters or employment agencies. In the absence of a signed Recruitment Fee Agreement, Kleinfelder, and its’ subsidiaries and affiliates, will not consider or agree to payment for any referral compensation or recruiter fee. In the event a recruiter or agency submits a resume or candidate without a previously signed agreement, Kleinfelder, and its’ subsidiaries and affiliates, expl

jobs.title: Public Outreach Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Job Description: **Take Your Career to the Next Level**
Kleinfelder, a Forbes' Top Employer for Engineers in North America, is committed to a people\-first culture, technical excellence, and creating opportunities where employees can thrive! **Step into Your New Role**
Kleinfelder Construction Services is seeking a full\-time **Public Outreach** **Project Coordinator** to join our Public Relations (PR) Team!

  



The **Public Outreach Project Coordinator** is a full\-time position that performs a variety of tasks for public outreach and stakeholder engagement for multiple contracts and communications programs. The Project Coordinator will work with the PR team to ensure smooth running of the division in daily tasks and as it relates to the entire firm. The ideal candidate will have a bachelor’s degree in public relations, communications, or journalism. The successful candidate will bring excellent writing, research, and organizational skills, adapt easily to a wide range of client needs, and consistently go above and beyond to deliver high\-quality work on time.

 **RESPONSIBILITIES**

* Collaborate with our team of public outreach specialists and graphic designers on he high\-level stragey, development, and implementation of communications programs and outreach plans for various clients.
* Draft written content for various traditional and digital collateral materials (e\-blast, fact sheets, postcards, construction alerts), including social media content
* Coordinate with technical teams, as needed, to gather project information for public dissemination
* Engage stakeholder audiences in person or online, maintaining the client’s messaging
* Establish and coordinate as\-needed vendor services such as translations, canvassing, printing, event logistics and supplies
* Collaborate with team on technology tools such as Smartsheet, Google Analytics, Constant Contact, CivicPlus, ArcGIS, Adobe Suite, Canva, Survey Monkey, Content Management Systems, etc. to communicate or manage project information
* Manage social media and digital advertising platforms for clients
* Document all outreach metrics and prepare monthly reports for KCS and clients
* Assist with research and ascertainment pertaining to planning
* Respond to requests for information from the media or designate another appropriate information source
* Establish and maintain cooperative relationships with representatives of community, employee, and public interest groups
* Manage/maintain databases and project helplines
* Create and distribute press releases and information alerts
* Assist in planning and implementation of milestone events
* Develop specific goals and plans to prioritize, organize and accomplish work
* Represent the company or clients at public meetings when necessary
* Maintain good inter\-departmental communication and a positive relationship with employees to promote employee satisfaction
* Assist with preparing developing and/or producing PR proposals in response to client Request for Proposals (RFP) or Statement of Qualifications (SOQ)
* Collaborate with Marketing in the production and delivery of PR proposals
* Perform office administration tasks
* Perform other duties as needed

 **BUILDING AND SUPPORTING TEAMS**

* Shows enthusiasm toward being a member of the group.
* Actively participates in team meetings and activities.
* Has a can\-do attitude and will do whatever it takes to get the job done
* Supports the team in all activities both internal and external.
* Ability to flourish in an environment of constant change
* Highly developed teamwork skills

 **JOB DESCRIPTION**

* Must be comfortable working with the public
* Coordination Activities
* Communicates project goals, timelines, and milestones.
* Ability to be a self\-starter, detail oriented and well organized
* Communicates issues with management and direct junior staff as needed
* Identifies project interdependencies and potential resource constraints.
* Follows up with others to ensure they are on track to meet project goals.
* Must be able to coordinate and track multiple tasks for concurrent projects, keeping each deadline and project goal on schedule
* Must be flexible and available to attend meetings or events during working and evening hours, including weekends

**COMMUNICATION SKILLS**

* Ability to thrive in team\-oriented environment
* Strong, effective communicator
* Exceptional writer and strong verbal communications skills
* Comfortable interacting with people at all levels of the organization.
* Adjusts style of conversation based on the audience.
* Uses words and messages appropriate given the background and interests of the people he/she is talking to.

 **EXPERIENCE \& ACADEMIC REQUIREMENTS**

**Education:** Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Public Relations or equivalent in related field required.

 **Experience:** 5\+ years related progressively complex experience, preferably with public relations experience in consulting engineering firm, public agency or related service business.

 **Other Knowledge and Skills:** Demonstrated experience working in social media; Facebook, X, Instagram, YouTube, LinkedIn, NextDoor, Google\+, blogs and other social networking sites preferred. Demonstrated ability to contribue to high\-level communications and campaign strategies. Must have excellent written and verbal communication skills. High\-level proficiency in Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and Excel. Must be highly organized and have strong desire to work in a team\-driven atmosphere. Strong research and data analysis skills preferred. Must be able to clearly articulate ideas and concepts verbally, graphically, and in writing.

  

Following an offer, candidates will be required to satisfactorily complete employment reference checks, verification of professional licensure and/or educational credentials, a motor vehicle report (MVR), and a comprehensive background check. **Move Forward with Kleinfelder:** Kleinfelder and its’ subsidi

jobs.title: Environmental Field Technician
jobs.description:
**The Position**

Field Technicians form the backbone of Blaine Tech. Their daily efforts and accomplishments on client sites are the single greatest factor in our continued success.

Successful Technicians are able to drive and operate our sampling vehicles safely (no special license required); operate on\-board generators, compressors and electric and pneumatic pumps; care for and handle test equipment and operate instruments correctly; execute prescribed protocols properly and consistently and; adhere to and enforce health \& safety mandates and requirements.

Technicians work outdoors; communicate regularly from the field; write legibly; collect, document and report scientific data truthfully, accurately and impartially; read and absorb technical information; work independently and; work as a productive member of a team.

We provide all of the required training. You provide the energy, interest and willingness to learn.

**Qualifications**

* Associate or Bachelor’s degree preferred. Science related degree and outdoor work experience a plus!
* Valid driver’s license and acceptable DMV record required.

Note: Blaine Tech is not currently requiring proof of COVID\-19 vaccination. However, as federal contractor subject to federal mandates, Blaine Tech may require proof of COVID\-19 vaccination in the future.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $21\.00 \- $25\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Referral program
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist/Fish Biologist
jobs.description:
Environmental Scientist/Fish Biologist

**Introduction:**

Point Environmental LLC is a small consulting firm based in Oregon City which helps its clients maintain compliance with environmental regulations in the construction and resource management industries throughout Oregon and Washington. The company is looking for a full\-time **Environmental Scientist/Fish Biologist** (Bio) to serve primarily in our southern service area. The southern service area extends from Oregon City to Eugene, and the company would prefer to hire an individual who currently resides between Oregon City and Salem.

The Bio will be assigned equipment, a company vehicle, and will be expected to report to jobsites or the Oregon City office directly from their residence the majority of the time. Therefore, their residence should be in the southern service area but within comfortable commuting distance of the Oregon City office at 914 Molalla Ave, Oregon City, OR 97045\.

The Bio’s primary responsibilities will involve providing environmental permit compliance and construction site erosion and sediment control inspection. The Bio’s other duties will include various environmental compliance tasks, particularly assisting with fish rescue/salvage operations during the summer in\-water work period, ESA species compliance, CWA compliance, and NEPA compliance.

The Bio is expected to work normal hours from 8am to 5pm Monday through Friday, but within that time, will be largely managing their own schedule which varies depending on weather conditions and project activities. Therefore, the individual needs to be self\-motivated and highly dependable.

**Responsibilities include:**

· Travel to multiple sites in Oregon, Washington, and occasionally Idaho

· Construction site inspection

· Fish salvage

· Remote stream survey

· Log entries using written and digital formats

· Thorough photo documentation

· Record keeping

· Report writing

· Communication with superintendents, foreman, and clients

· Spending long periods of time on your feet walking in inclement weather and rough terrain

**Job Requirements:**

· Bachelor's degree in fish biology, environmental science, or similar field

· At least **one (1\) of the** following Oregon DEQ acceptable erosion and sediment control inspection certifications:

o Certified Professional in Erosion and Sediment Control (CPESC)

o Certified Professional in Storm Water Quality (CPSWQ)

o Certified Erosion and Sediment Control Lead (CESCL)

o Rogue Valley Sewer Services Erosion and Sediment Control Certification

· Minimum 1 year experience (Please Include a List of Projects)

· Valid driver’s license

· Need to provide own weather appropriate clothing including above the ankle leather work boots. Point Environmental will provide the necessary safety PPE.

· Must currently have reliable transportation.

· A motivated and organized individual with honesty, integrity, who wants to work, and who is willing to learn and share their individuality and ideas to better the company.

· Construction casual attire and appearance, e.g. jeans or kakis with collard shirt or Point Environmental T\-shirt.

· Experience with Microsoft Office Suite and Adobe Acrobat Pro

**Job Preferences:**

· Preference will be given to those individuals with an educational emphasis and additional work experience in fish biology, electrofishing, forestry, civil construction, QGIS, etc.

**Compensation:**

The Bio will be an “at will” employee who will receive an hourly compensation commensurate with experience.

**Hiring Schedule:**

Immediately. Interviews will be scheduled with acceptable candidates.

**Benefits:**

Health/Dental insurance is available for employees working 30 hours a week or more.

Paid time off

Remote work

Company vehicle or paid mileage

Career training

Self\-management, flexible

Small company, relaxed atmosphere

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $25\.00 \- $30\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person
